steps.title: Undo the coefficient
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by $-\frac{25}{9}$:
$$-\frac{25}{9}(D-T)=100-H$$
The left side can also be written as $\frac{25}{9}(T-D)$.

steps.title: Solve for H
steps.content:
Subtract $100$ from both sides and then multiply by $-1$:
$$H=100+\frac{25}{9}(D-T)$$
So the equivalent expression is:
$$H=\frac{25}{9}(D-T)+100$$

steps.title: Check the answer choice with sample values
steps.content:
Use quick sample values to catch sign errors. If $T=80$ and $H=75$, then the original formula gives $D=80-\frac{9}{25}(25)=71$. Plugging $D=71$ and $T=80$ into the correct expression gives:
$$\frac{25}{9}(71-80)+100=75$$
It returns the original $H$, so A is correct.
